intent: Pin the dark mode contract: Light/Dark/System with persistence; status colors retain semantic meaning; tables/graph/warnings stay readable across both themes.

cases.description: Toggle between Light/Dark/System and confirm the html.dark class is added/removed.
cases.expected_outcome: Dark click ⇒ html.dark added; Light click ⇒ removed.
cases.actual_outcome: verified by ThemeToggle.test.tsx:'selecting Dark adds .dark to <html>' and 'selecting Light removes .dark'

cases.description: Selection persists across mounts via localStorage[d3l-theme].
cases.expected_outcome: Stored preference reapplied on next render.
cases.actual_outcome: verified by ThemeToggle.test.tsx:'reads stored preference on mount'

cases.description: Status colors (red/orange/yellow/green/gray) preserve semantic meaning under both themes.
cases.expected_outcome: STATUS_CLASSES carry text/bg/border with dark: variants in the same color family.
cases.actual_outcome: verified by theme.test.ts:'STATUS_CLASSES preserves semantic colors across themes'

cases.description: Trust state mapping is registry-aligned (blocked_signal⇒red, freeze_signal⇒orange, caution_signal⇒yellow, ready_signal⇒green, unknown⇒gray).
cases.expected_outcome: semanticColorForTrustState returns the canonical color per state.

cases.description: On first paint with dark preference set, the page must not flash light mode.
cases.expected_outcome: Pre-hydration script in layout.tsx <head> applies html.dark before React mounts.
cases.actual_outcome: Implemented as inline THEME_BOOTSTRAP script in apps/dashboard-3ls/app/layout.tsx; reads localStorage and matchMedia synchronously.

fixes_applied.rationale: Pure helpers (resolveTheme, STATUS_CLASSES, semanticColorForTrustState) so theme logic is fully testable without DOM globals.
Light/Dark/System component wired into the global Navigation. Persists in localStorage and respects matchMedia for system preference.
Tailwind class strategy + pre-hydration script avoids flash-of-light. Body uses bg/text classes with dark variants so the entire app picks up the theme.
